description: In January 2024, Lai Ching-te of the DPP won the Taiwanese presidential election against Hou Yu-ih and Ko Wen-je. Despite heavy pressure and regional tensions, Lai secured 40.05% in a three-way race, which functions as a binary outcome when viewed as support vs. opposition to the ruling party platform.

key_events.description: Opposition fails to form a joint coalition ticket
Increased regional military activity heightens security discourse
Final campaign rallies show heavy mobilization

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling scenarios where the 'winning' side has a plurality but faces a combined opposition majority, common in multi-party systems.
